heroHeadline: SAF Eliminates Senior RSF Commander in Central Darfur; Dilling Road Reopened After Siege Operations
heroSubtitle: SAF drone strike killed Hamid Ali Abubakar — commander of the RSF-aligned Sudanese Awakening Revolutionary Council (RAC) and security adviser to Hemedti — at a command meeting in Damrat al-Firdous, Central Darfur on April 8, one of the highest-value targeted kills in the Darfur theater; SAF simultaneously secured Al-Tukma (7km east of Dilling) and reopened the critical Dilling–North Kordofan supply road, breaking the RSF-SPLM-N partial siege of Dilling; Berlin Conference preparatory talks confirmed for Addis Ababa April 10–12 ahead of the April 15 ministerial conference in Berlin, with boycott threats from multiple Sudanese political factions remaining a core challenge to the process
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia
